Reasoning: The patient's current hospital visit record is dominated by severe infectious and inflammatory processes, including severe sepsis, septic shock, and acute kidney failure. The diagnoses made are: Other and unspecified infection due to central venous catheter; Other septicemia due to gram-negative organisms; Severe sepsis; Septic shock; Acute kidney failure, unspecified; Other pulmonary embolism and infarction; Unspecified protein-calorie malnutrition; Other and unspecified postsurgical nonabsorption; Ostium secundum type atrial septal defect; Stricture and stenosis of esophagus; Reflux esophagitis; Leukocytopenia, unspecified; Anemia, unspecified; Ileostomy status. The medication profile is heavily focused on broad-spectrum antibiotics (Ciprofloxacin IV, Meropenem, Daptomycin, CeftriaXONE, Fluconazole), supportive care (1/2 NS, 0.9% Sodium Chloride, 5% Dextrose, Magnesium Sulfate, Potassium Chloride), and management of shock/sepsis (Heparin, Micafungin, Fentanyl Patch, Ondansetron). The procedures performed are related to infection management and nutritional support (Venous catheterization, Parenteral infusion of concentrated nutritional substances, Diagnostic ultrasound of heart, Other endoscopy of small intestine).
